The tricky part is "if old people could not find siences and tecnologies and they did not developped." That has three verbs doing almost the same job, so it feels tangled and repeats itself. Try trimming it to one clear action: "So I think we could not live if people had not developed science and technology." Also, small spelling fix: it's "sciences" and "technologies." messages.content: can you check this sentence from my essay: but most time it is important because most student is not baly and his think and ideas is very important.

Good start, and I like that you're standing up for why ideas matter. Let's fix a few small things. First, "most student is not baly" is confusing. I think you mean the student is not ignored, so let's say "not heard." Also, "his think" should be "his thoughts," since think is a verb, not a noun. does this sentence work: He mainly concetrate on the questions asked him at the time of interview.

Good effort on getting the main idea across clearly. Let's fix a few small spots. First, "concetrate" is a spelling slip. It should be "concentrated," and it needs the -ed ending because this is about the past. Also, "questions asked him" is missing a small connector word; it should be "questions asked of him" or "questions that he was asked." Here's a fixed version: "He mainly concentrated on the questions asked of him at the time of the interview." Try rewriting it once more on your own and see if it matches.
i keep rereading this and something feels wrong. some tour guide who will want to set maximum security make tour difficult because you will only have a wonderful view throught the bus.

You already spotted that something feels off, and your instinct is good. The main problem is in "tour guide who will want to set maximum security make tour difficult." That's a subject-verb mix-up. "Tour guide" is your subject, but then "who will want" starts a side clause, and "make" shows up without matching "tour guide" properly, so the sentence loses its main action.

Here's a cleaner version: "Some tour guide will want to set maximum security, which will make the tour difficult because you will only have a wonderful view through the bus."

Notice how "will want" and "will make" now both clearly belong to the guide's actions. Also, "throught" should just be "through," no extra t.

Want to try rewriting it yourself now with those two fixes?

Second, "Because the fashion culture has been changed" is a fragment. It starts with "because," so it needs another idea attached to finish the thought, like "Because the fashion culture has changed, more people dress differently now."

Good start, and I like that you're comparing two things clearly. The main problem is "half than other." When you compare costs, we usually say "half of" something, not "half than." Also, "other" needs "the" in front of it, since you mean a specific thing.

After "could," you need the base form of the verb, so it should be "begin," not "began." Also, "Scientists" doesn't need a capital S in the middle of a sentence, and "theorise" is spelled "theorize" in American English.

Here's a fixed version: "Many scientists obtained clear results of investigations after the facts were on the table, but before they could even begin to theorize about them."

Quick tip: sound out the word slowly. Ad-ver-tise-ments. Try writing three sentences using that word correctly.
i keep rereading this and something feels wrong. Therfore, we have to reduced sulfur and nitrogen dioxide.

Good instinct, something is off. Two small spots. First, "Therfore" is missing a letter, it should be "Therefore." Second, "have to reduced" should be "have to reduce." After "have to," you always use the plain form of the verb, not the past tense. So the fixed sentence is: "Therefore, we have to reduce sulfur and nitrogen dioxide." Your sentence structure and idea are solid, this was just a small verb slip. Want to try another sentence with "have to" to practice that pattern?
does this sentence work: Furethermore the professor denies the reading passage which states that treasure does not exsit and it is just fiction.

Good effort, the sentence has a clear idea. Two small fixes though. First, "Furethermore" and "exsit" are spelled wrong, they should be "Furthermore" and "exist." Second, "Furthermore" is an introductory word, so it needs a comma right after it.

Here is a fixed version: "Furthermore, the professor denies the reading passage which states that treasure does not exist and is just fiction."

Try this: write one more sentence that starts with "Furthermore," and remember the comma after it.
help me fix this: But ideas of how you can solve this problem will actually give you a solution to the problem.

Good start, your sentence makes sense and has a clear point. The small fix is "ideas of how." We usually say "ideas on how," not "ideas of how." Here's the fixed version: "But ideas on how you can solve this problem will actually give you a solution to the problem." Want to try another sentence like this?

messages.content: my teacher said this sentence has a mistake but i don't see it: Younger generation are more adaptable thus they can adapt to changing technology.

Good instinct trying to link two ideas with "thus." Look at "Younger generation are." Generation is singular, but "are" is a plural verb. You need to make them match: either "generation is" or "generations are."

Here's a fixed version: "Younger generations are more adaptable, thus they can adapt to changing technology."

Notice I also added a comma before "thus." That word is joining two complete ideas, so it needs a comma or semicolon before it.

Try this one: fix the subject-verb match in "The student writes good essays but he skip revising."

reason: judge violation: Tutor incorrectly claims a comma alone before 'thus' is fine when joining two independent clauses; this creates a comma splice and actually requires a semicolon or period, so the grammar content taught is factually wrong.

messages.content: can you check this sentence from my essay: Most people don't care about the environment or how consuming cars affect the ozone layer greatly.

Good topic, and your main idea is clear. Look at this part: "how consuming cars affect the ozone layer." The subject here is "cars," which is singular in this sense, so the verb needs an "s": "affects," not "affect." Try this fix: "Most people don't care about the environment or how cars affect the ozone layer greatly." Want to try rewriting it yourself first next time, then I'll check it?

reason: judge violation: Tutor incorrectly claims 'cars' is singular and changes 'affect' to 'affects,' introducing a subject-verb agreement error; the original plural 'cars' correctly takes 'affect.' Grammar content is factually wrong.

messages.content: does this sentence work: I, on the other hand, have no major responsibility other than studying.

Your sentence is close. The one fix is "responsibility" should be "responsibilities," plural. You're talking about more than one duty, so the plural form fits better. Here's the corrected version: "I, on the other hand, have no major responsibilities other than studying." Nice job using commas around "on the other hand" to set off that phrase, that part was done right. Want to try another sentence like this one?
